summary: Ukrainian forces intensified UAV activity across multiple fronts, with guided bomb strikes reported in Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk. Russia reported downing two UAVs en route to Moscow and highlighted a chemical weapons incident on the front line. International support continued with a US-approved $108 million sale of Hawk air‑defence equipment to Ukraine.
key_points: Ukrainian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia, targeting Russian positions (Source: @kpszsu/62428).
Russian air defence shot down two UAVs heading toward Moscow, according to Moscow mayor (Source: @tass_agency/377124).

Multiple UAV sightings reported across Chernihiv, Sumy, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk and Kirovohrad regions, indicating heightened drone activity (Source: @kpszsu/62399, @kpszsu/62419, @kpszsu/62420, @kpszsu/62431, @kpszsu/62455, @kpszsu/62445).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV clusters over Konotop, Poltava, Pavlohrad and Vasylkivka, suggesting coordinated strikes (Source: @eRadarrua/93009).
Russia condemned the Ukrainian strike on a college in Starobilsk, labeling it a terrorist act and prompting UN discussions (Source: @tass_agency/377141, @tass_agency/377128).
Ukraine announced technical readiness to evacuate approximately 6,000 civilians from the Oleshok area pending a ceasefire (Source: @UkraineNow/81381).
Ukraine reported over 13,300 incidents of chemical weapon use along the front line, calling for increased G7 support (Source: @UkraineNow/81380).
The United States approved a $108 million sale of Hawk anti‑aircraft missile system equipment to Ukraine (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30358).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian tactical aviation deployed guided bombs in Zaporizhzhia, intensifying strikes on Russian positions. Russia reported downing two UAVs bound for Moscow and highlighted a surge in chemical weapon incidents on the front. International aid continued with a US‑approved $108 million Hawk air‑defence package for Ukraine.

points: Ukrainian forces launched guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia, targeting Russian defensive positions and causing material damage (Source: @kpszsu/62428).

Multiple UAV sightings were reported in Chernihiv (north), Sumy (north), Kharkiv (south‑east), Dnipropetrovsk (south‑east) and Kirovohrad (south‑east), indicating expanded drone operations across the northern and eastern fronts (Source: @kpszsu/62399, @kpszsu/62419, @kpszsu/62420, @kpszsu/62431, @kpszsu/62455, @kpszsu/62445).
Ukrainian intelligence posted clusters of UAVs over Konotop, Poltava, Pavlohrad and Vasylkivka, suggesting coordinated strike waves (Source: @eRadarrua/93009).
Ukraine declared technical readiness to evacuate roughly 6,000 civilians, including 200 children, from the Oleshok area pending a ceasefire agreement (Source: @UkraineNow/81381).
Ukraine reported more than 13,300 chemical weapon incidents along the front line, urging G7 partners to enhance protective and response capabilities (Source: @UkraineNow/81380).
The United States approved a $108 million sale of Hawk air‑defence system equipment to Ukraine, aiming to bolster its air‑defence network (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30358).
Russian air‑defence forces shot down two UAVs en route to Moscow, as confirmed by Mayor Sobyanin (Source: @tass_agency/377124).
Russia condemned the Ukrainian strike on a college in Starobilsk, labeling it a terrorist act and calling for UN action (Source: @tass_agency/377141).
Russian officials highlighted a fire at an oil depot in Novorossiysk caused by falling UAV debris, indicating UAV threats to rear areas (Source: @tass_agency/377144).
Russia reported the use of guided aviation bombs by Ukrainian tactical aviation in Zaporizhzhia, describing them as hostile aerial attacks (Source: @kpszsu/62428).
Russian statements emphasized that Ukrainian attacks on civilian infrastructure, such as the Starobilsk college, constitute war crimes (Source: @tass_agency/377128).
Russia noted increased chemical weapon usage by Ukrainian forces, framing it as a new threat to Russian troops (Source: @UkraineNow/81380).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed moving toward Pavlograd in Dnipropetrovsk region, indicating pressure on Russian supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/62431).
Guided bomb launches were reported from Ukrainian tactical aviation targeting positions in Donetsk and eastern Dnipropetrovsk, suggesting intensified strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/62436).
UAV clusters over Konotop and Pavlohrad point to coordinated attacks on eastern logistical hubs (Source: @eRadarrua/93009).
Russian forces reported downing two UAVs heading to Moscow, reflecting broader air‑defence activity that may affect eastern airspace (Source: @tass_agency/377124).
Chemical weapon incidents were reported across the eastern front, with Ukrainian authorities documenting over 13,300 cases (Source: @UkraineNow/81380).
Ukrainian forces continue to report UAV activity in Kharkiv oblast, moving toward villages such as Barvinkove and Sofiyivka (Source: @eRadarrua/92994).
UAVs detected over Chernihiv heading south toward Mena, prompting civilian shelter alerts (Source: @kpszsu/62399).
UAV activity reported in Sumy and Chernihiv northern regions, indicating Russian‑aligned reconnaissance or attacks (Source: @kpszsu/62455).
UAV sightings near Romny in Sumy, moving toward Digtiary‑Silverne, suggest cross‑border drone incursions (Source: @kpszsu/62445).
Russian air‑defence downed two UAVs bound for Moscow, reflecting heightened air‑defence posture that may affect northern air corridors (Source: @tass_agency/377124).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV clusters over Shostka, prompting local civilian sheltering (Source: @kpszsu/62419).
No major ground engagements reported, but UAV presence indicates ongoing surveillance and limited strike capability on the northern front.
Ukrainian guided bomb strikes were reported in Zaporizhzhia, targeting Russian positions in the south (Source: @kpszsu/62428).
UAV activity noted in Kirovohrad region heading toward Kropyvnytskyi, suggesting pressure on Russian rear areas (Source: @kpszsu/62420).
UAV sightings over the southern part of Romny (Sumy) moving toward Chernihiv indicate potential southern‑northern axis operations (Source: @kpszsu/62445).
No direct reports of large‑scale ground combat, but UAV and guided bomb activity imply continued Ukrainian offensive posture in the south.
Chemical weapon incident reports include southern sectors, highlighting the spread of alleged prohibited weapons use (Source: @UkraineNow/81380).
Russian statements claim Ukrainian attacks on civilian infrastructure in the south, labeling them as terrorist acts (Source: @tass_agency/377141).
Russian air‑defence shot down two UAVs heading to Moscow, demonstrating effective interception capability (Source: @tass_agency/377124).
Ukrainian forces deployed guided aviation bombs from tactical aircraft over Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk, indicating expanded air‑strike capacity (Source: @kpszsu/62428, @kpszsu/62436).

Multiple UAV clusters reported across northern, eastern and southern fronts, suggesting a coordinated drone campaign by Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/62399, @kpszsu/62419, @kpszsu/62420, @eRadarrua/93009).
UAV debris caused a fire at an oil depot in Novorossiysk, highlighting the risk of UAV strikes on Russian logistical sites (Source: @tass_agency/377144).
The US sale of Hawk air‑defence equipment to Ukraine aims to improve its ability to counter Russian UAV and missile threats (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30358).
Russia accused Ukraine of using chemical weapons, while Ukraine reported Russian chemical attacks, indicating a contested narrative in the air‑defence domain (Source: @UkraineNow/81380).

key_developments: Ukrainian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia, striking Russian defensive positions (Source: @kpszsu/62428).
Russian air‑defence forces downed two UAVs en route to Moscow, confirming active interception (Source: @tass_agency/377124).

Multiple UAV sightings across Chernihiv, Sumy, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk and Kirovohrad indicate a broad Ukrainian drone campaign (Source: @kpszsu/62399, @kpszsu/62419, @kpszsu/62420, @kpszsu/62431, @kpszsu/62455, @kpszsu/62445).
Ukraine reported over 13,300 chemical weapon incidents along the front, urging G7 support for protective measures (Source: @UkraineNow/81380).
The United States approved a $108 million sale of Hawk air‑defence system equipment to Ukraine (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30358).
Russia condemned the Ukrainian strike on a college in Starobilsk, labeling it a terrorist act and prompting UN discussions (Source: @tass_agency/377141).
Ukraine announced technical readiness to evacuate approximately 6,000 civilians from the Oleshok area pending a ceasefire (Source: @UkraineNow/81381).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian forces are expanding their aerial strike capabilities, employing both guided bombs and a dense network of UAVs across multiple fronts, which pressures Russian rear areas and logistics. Russia maintains effective air‑defence, demonstrated by intercepting UAVs bound for Moscow, but faces vulnerability to drone incursions and guided bomb attacks, especially in Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk. The reported chemical weapon incidents, if verified, could introduce a new escalation factor, while the US Hawk system sale will likely enhance Ukrainian air‑defence resilience.
sections.regional_response: The United States' approval of Hawk system equipment underscores continued Western military support, aiming to counter the growing UAV threat. Russia's diplomatic narrative frames Ukrainian attacks as terrorist acts, seeking to rally international condemnation and justify harsher measures. Ukraine's evacuation readiness in Oleshok reflects a strategic focus on civilian protection amid intensified combat operations.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths should prioritize Chernihiv‑Mena corridor and the Dnipropetrovsk‑Pavlograd axis, where repeated sightings suggest operational patterns. Guided bomb deployments indicate Ukrainian tactical aviation is gaining confidence in deep‑strike missions; SIGINT should target launch coordinates for future targeting. Verification of chemical weapon use remains critical, as both sides leverage the narrative for propaganda and potential escalation.
